description: FTX collapse in November 2022 dramatically shifted opinion on crypto regulation. Pro-regulation sentiment jumped from ~45% to ~65% as $8B in customer funds vanished. Sam Bankman-Fried arrested. Tests scandal-driven regulatory opinion shift and trust collapse.

key_events.description: Terra/Luna stablecoin collapse (May 2022) wipes out $40B. First major crack in crypto confidence. Regulation talk increases.
Celsius, Voyager, and Three Arrows Capital collapse in cascade. 'Crypto winter' deepens. Congressional hearings begin.
FTX files bankruptcy (Nov 11, 2022). $8B customer funds missing. Sam Bankman-Fried's fraud exposed. Massive shock to remaining crypto trust.
SBF arrested in Bahamas, extradited to US. SEC Chair Gensler calls crypto 'rife with fraud'. Bipartisan regulation momentum surges.
Congressional bills for crypto regulation multiply. Even crypto-friendly politicians shift toward oversight. Industry pivots to welcoming 'smart regulation'.

calibration_notes: Tests scandal-driven regulatory consensus formation. Key patterns: gradual pre-crisis drift toward regulation, cascade of failures (Luna, Celsius, FTX) each adding ~5-8pts, FTX as definitive shock event (+10pts in two weeks), anti-regulation position collapsed rather than pro-regulation growing (opposition dropped from 30% to 20%), industry itself pivoted from opposing to embracing regulation. Morning Consult, Pew Research, YouGov polling data used.
